epics.title: Remove UI clutter from songs index

tasks.title: Compress the songs page header
acceptance_criteria: The header removes redundant kicker, marketing-style headline copy, and separate stats block.
The page still exposes the main purpose and aggregate count in a compact form.
done_definition: src/app/songs/page.tsx renders a tighter internal-tool header.

tasks.title: Reduce song row visual clutter
acceptance_criteria: Long summaries are no longer always-visible in the list.
Metadata is consolidated into compact inline chunks.
Each row remains a keyboard-focusable link with title and artist visible.
done_definition: src/app/songs/song-list.tsx keeps accessible links while reducing prose and stacked labels.

tasks.title: Verify rendered songs cleanup
acceptance_criteria: Browser before and after evidence shows reduced UI clutter.
Representative rows render without redundant local labels or avoidable horizontal overflow.
A remaining song detail route still opens successfully.
Lint, build, and run artifact validation pass.
done_definition: verify.json records terminal and browser evidence with no blocking issues.

assumptions: The UI weeds are redundant explanatory copy and overly verbose row content on the songs index.
The previous removal of the temporary sample should remain in place.
No new search, filters, or detail behavior are needed for this cleanup.
risks: Removing summaries from the list trades preview context for scanability; detail pages still carry full chart information.
The user may have a more specific visual weed in mind, but the current screenshot shows the most obvious clutter in header and rows.
